subject: ap-chemistry
title: Atomic Structure & Properties
stimulus:
Some binary compounds that form between fluorine and various nonmetals are listed in the table above. A
student examines the data in the table and poses the following hypothesis: the number of F atoms that will bond
to a nonmetal is always equal to 8 minus the number of valence electrons in the nonmetal atom.

parts.prompt:
Based on the student’s hypothesis, what should be the formula of the compound that forms between chlorine
and fluorine?

scoring_points.description: Provide a correct response for part (a)

alternatives.correct_example: Based on the student’s hypothesis, what should be the formula of the compound that forms between chlorine and fluorine? ClF

parts.prompt:
In an attempt to verify the hypothesis, the student researches the fluoride compounds of the other halogens
and finds the formula ClF
. In the box below, draw a complete Lewis electron-dot diagram for a molecule
of ClF
.

Two possible geometric shapes for the ClF
molecule are trigonal planar and T-shaped. The student does
some research and learns that the molecule has a dipole moment. Which of the two shapes is consistent with
the fact that the ClF
molecule has a dipole moment? Justify your answer in terms of bond polarity and
molecular structure.
In an attempt to resolve the existence of the ClF
molecule with the hypothesis stated above, the student
researches the compounds that form between halogens and fluorine, and assembles the following list.
Halogen Formula(s)
F F
Cl
Br BrF, BrF
, BrF
I IF, IF , IF , IF
3 5 7

scoring_points.description: State the correct answer with a valid chemical justification

required_elements: Valid justification based on chemical principles
alternatives.correct_example: Two possible geometric shapes for the ClF3 molecule are trigonal planar and T -shaped. The student does some research and learns that the molecule has a dipole moment. Which of the two shapes is consistent with the fact that the ClF3 molecule has a dipole moment? Justify your answer in terms of bond polarity and molecular structure. The molecule is T -shaped because a T -shaped structure is asymmetric with dipoles that do not cancel out, but produce a net dipole (i.e., a polar molecule). OR b...

common_traps: Stating answer without connecting to the underlying chemical principle

parts.prompt:
Based on concepts of atomic structure and periodicity, propose a modification to the student’s previous
hypothesis to account for the compounds that form between halogens and fluorine.

scoring_points.description: Provide a correct response for part (d)

alternatives.correct_example: Based on concepts of atomic structure and periodicity, propose a modification to the student’s previous hypothesis to account for the compounds that form between halogens and fluorine. An acceptable hypothesis (descriptive or formulaic) must include the following ideas: Atomic Structure: e.g., odd number of F atoms Periodicity: e.g., as the atomic number of the central halogen atom increases, the number of F atoms increases
